title: Device Trust & Posture
description: Verifying device health and compliance before granting network or application access.
fullExplanation: Zero Trust requires verifying not just who is connecting, but what device they're using. Device posture checks verify that the device meets security requirements: OS is up to date, disk is encrypted, EDR agent is running, no jailbreak/root. Only compliant devices are granted access. Non-compliant devices are quarantined or given limited access. This prevents compromised or unmanaged devices from accessing sensitive resources.

posturChecks.description: Ensure OS is current — block outdated Windows/macOS/iOS/Android
Verify critical patches applied — no known unpatched CVEs
Endpoint Detection & Response agent running and up to date
Detect rooted Android or jailbroken iOS — block access
Device has valid certificate issued by corporate CA

technologies.description: Manage and enforce policy on mobile devices. Examples: Jamf, Intune, VMware Workspace ONE.
Monitor endpoint for threats. Examples: CrowdStrike Falcon, SentinelOne, Microsoft Defender.
Check device posture before allowing network access. Examples: Cisco ISE, Aruba ClearPass.
Hardware chip that stores cryptographic keys — proves device identity.

keyFacts: BYOD risk: personal devices may not meet corporate security standards
Certificate-based device identity: device cert issued by corporate CA — proves managed device
Conditional Access: grant/deny access based on device compliance score
Continuous monitoring: re-evaluate device posture throughout session, not just at login
Quarantine VLAN: non-compliant devices get limited access to remediation resources only
Zero Trust Device: device must be known, managed, and compliant — not just any device
